water.name: Non-anadromous tributaries of the Klamath River above Lakeview Road bridge

authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(73) — Non-anadromous tributaries of the Klamath River above Lakeview Road bridge
sources.quotedText: Non-anadromous tributaries of the Klamath River above Lakeview Road bridge. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. 5 trout Oct. 1 through the Fri. preceding Memorial Day. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(a)(4) — General possession limit provision for listed trout waters
sources.quotedText: Unless otherwise provided, it is unlawful to possess more than one daily bag limit.

sources.title: American Whitewater — Klamath River, Iron Gate reach (Lakeview Road bridge area)
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: Wikipedia — Iron Gate Dam, Siskiyou County, California (infobox coordinates; used as the representative point for the Lakeview Road bridge boundary, since the bridge itself sits immediately downstream of the former dam site and neither "Lakeview Road" nor "Iron Gate Dam"/"Iron Gate Reservoir" returns a queryable record in the USGS GNIS ArcGIS gazetteer's Streams (MapServer/6), Other Hydrographic Features (MapServer/7), or Crossings (MapServer/10) layers for Siskiyou County, CA)
sources.quotedText: 41°56′02″N 122°26′07″W / 41.93389°N 122.43528°W = 41.93389, -122.43528. Corroborated by OpenStreetMap/Nominatim's point for "Iron Gate Dam" on Lakeview Road (41.9344, -122.4368) and by the Iron Gate Fish Hatchery's geocoded address at 8636 Lakeview Road, Hornbrook, CA (41.9296, -122.4420), both within ~600m. The file's coordinate prior to this audit (-122.42, 41.95) was off by roughly 2.2 km; corrected to this cluster.

regulations.humanSummary: Two-period season: take season from the Saturday preceding Memorial Day through September 30, then catch-and-release from October 1 through the Friday preceding Memorial Day
regulations.verbatimText: Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. 5 trout Oct. 1 through the Fri. preceding Memorial Day. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout

parameters.counting_rule: 5 trout daily bag limit during the summer take season (Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30); possession limit equals one daily bag limit (5) per the general provision at 14 CCR §7.50(a)(4) absent a separately stated possession limit.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit during the Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30 take season
regulations.verbatimText: Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. 5 trout

regulations.humanSummary: 0 trout, catch-and-release only, from Oct. 1 through the Friday preceding Memorial Day
regulations.verbatimText: Oct. 1 through the Fri. preceding Memorial Day. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout

parameters.note: Applies during the Oct. 1 through Friday-preceding-Memorial-Day catch-and-release period only.

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used, Oct. 1 through the Friday preceding Memorial Day
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
